content:
Sam, 31, backend/ML engineer at a Paris logistics startup — debriefing the Lisbon trip (trip dates 2026-04-28 to 2026-05-02) on 2026-05-06. Quick facts first: flights return with TAP, €118 total; Airbnb 4 nights in Alfama €360; food/coffee €158; local transport (Viva Viagem top-ups) €42; extras (museum tickets, souvenirs, charger) €92. Grand total ≈ €770.

What worked: walking-heavy plan worked — averaged 10–12 km/day (phone says 48 km total), Tram 28 experience was worth it, Belém pastries (Pastéis de Belém €3 each) and Taberna da Rua das Flores dinner on 2026-04-29 was the best meal — €46 pp including wine. MAAT on 2026-04-30 was beautiful and €11 entry prebooked was painless. The compact base in Alfama meant I could drop into cafes between walks.

What didn’t: Airbnb Wi‑Fi was flaky (dropouts 2–3 times/day), no dedicated desk so I had to work one afternoon on the bed; I forgot my USB‑C charger and bought a replacement for €25; phone battery died twice because I didn’t bring the power bank (ended up buying one €45); tourist crowds around Rossio felt overpriced for food; tram queues long midday. Also one minor pickpocket attempt near Praça do Comércio — only loss was stress.

Concrete decisions to test next time: 1) prioritize an Airbnb or hotel listing that explicitly mentions 'workspace' and >100 Mbps — willing to pay +€30/night (that’s +€120 for 4 nights). 2) Buy an eSIM before departure (€15 for 5 days from Holafly or Airalo). 3) Pack spare USB‑C and a 20,000 mAh power bank (budget €45). 4) Prebook 1–2 restaurants and 1 key museum; leave 2 relaxed walk-only days. 5) Buy a 24‑hour transport pass on day 1 (€6.80) to avoid repeated top-ups. 6) Set total target budget ≈ €720 next time (tight but includes the workspace premium).

Decisions still open: do I prebook a co‑working day as backup (cost ≈ €12–€15), or just rely on a 'business-ready' listing + café backup? Also, which city for the next short trip — Porto, Barcelona, or Naples — factoring travel time and per-day costs?
Noted: Wi‑Fi/chargers are the biggest frictions and budget will rise if you want a true workspace. Which single change would you prioritize next trip?

Prioritize reliable Wi‑Fi and a workspace. Practically that means: filter listings for the word 'workspace' and at least one recent review mentioning 'fast Wi‑Fi' or 'good for working'. If average Airbnb in Lisbon was €90/night, I accept up to €120/night for the workspace listing — that's the +€30/night. That brings accommodation to ≈€480 for 4 nights versus €360 this trip.

Also: buy an eSIM before departure (estimate €15), pack/buy spare USB‑C (€15 online or cheaper if I already have one), buy a 20,000 mAh power bank (€45) — addables ≈€75. If I add one co‑working day as backup €12, total extra above last trip ≈ €402, so new target budget ≈ €1,172? Wait — recalc: last trip ≈€770; +€120 (airbnb) +€75 (gear) +€12 (cowork) = +€207 → target ≈ €977. I need to set a realistic upper bound: okay cap at €1,000 for a 4‑night city break if I want work convenience.

Schedule tweaks: pick arrival midday to avoid early morning stress (so book flights leaving Paris after 08:00). Prebook Taberna da Rua das Flores for dinner if I want to repeat the experience — reservations open around 2–3 weeks in advance. For museums, only prebook 1 paid site (MAAT or Serralves in Porto later). Keep two fully free days.

Logistics: buy a small crossbody money belt to avoid another pickpocket scenario (€18). Pack a lightweight rain jacket — it rained on 2026-05-01 and ruined an afternoon walk. I’ll keep the Tram 28 but go early to avoid queues (pre-09:30).
Good — workspace + basic gear sounds consistent. For the co‑working backup, would you prefer to prebook a single day or buy a flexible drop‑in on the spot?

Prebook one co‑working day as backup. Reason: I had to do ~4 hours of work on 2026-04-30 and didn’t want to gamble with finding a seat. Price estimate: €12 for a day pass (CoworkLisbon or HedenLabs). If I don’t use it, I’ll cancel or skip.

Next, concrete packing checklist (to actually put in my suitcase): USB‑C spare, 20,000 mAh power bank, 1x universal travel adapter (I have one), lightweight waterproof jacket, money belt, quick‑dry socks, a small microfiber towel. Also set phone to offline maps + download 2 restaurant PDFs and confirmation emails. Buy eSIM 48 hours before departure.

Budget plan for next trip with workspace and one cowork day: flights €120 (book 6 weeks out), accommodation €480, food €160, transport €30, extras (museum, co‑working, eSIM, charger/power bank) €95 → target €985. I’ll round up to €1,000 as the safety cap.

Open question resolved: workspace > co‑working if budget forced to choose one. But I’ll book both (workspace listing + 1 co‑working day) this time.
Clear checklist and cap at €1,000. Which city are you leaning toward for the next break, given those constraints?

Porto. Reasons: 1) Flight time ~1h30 from Paris, cheaper fares (I found returns around €60–€80 when booked ~6 weeks out), 2) similar walking/cobbled streets vibe so the same shoes/packing works, 3) wine + food scene I want to revisit, and 4) Serralves museum is on my list.

Proposed dates: 2026-09-10 to 2026-09-14 (4 nights). Plan checklist for Porto trip: 
- Book flights as soon as 6 weeks prior (target €70 return). 
- Book Airbnb with 'workspace' tag, budget €110/night → €440. 
- Prebuy Holafly/Airalo eSIM €15. 
- Buy power bank €45 + spare USB‑C €15. 
- Prebook 1 museum (Serralves) and 1 key dinner (reserve Taberna-style meal). 
- Prebook 1 co‑working day €12. 
- Target total ≈ €680–€750; with buffer cap at €900 (Porto should be cheaper than Lisbon).

Lessons carried forward from Lisbon: always have backup power, prebook one work option, leave 2 free days, avoid tourist traps near main squares for dinner, arrive midday, and check weather 3 days before and pack light rain jacket. Also update packing checklist into my phone notes by 2026-08-20.
Solid plan with dates and price targets. Want me to draft the exact booking checklist with links, cancellation windows, and a reminder schedule (e.g., flights T‑6 weeks, eSIM T‑48h)?
